mnemonics: I Saw Five Minions Counting Nuts (Insertion, Searching, Find Min/Max, Count Nodes)
keyPoints: Insertion requires finding the correct position to maintain data structure order
Searching algorithms vary in efficiency (O(n) for linear, O(log n) for binary search in balanced trees)
Minimum value is found at the leftmost node, maximum at the rightmost node in BSTs
Node counting requires traversing the entire structure and incrementing a counter
commonMistakes: Forgetting to update pointers/references during insertion, causing data loss
Assuming a binary search tree is balanced when implementing search operations
Not handling edge cases (empty structure, single node) when finding min/max values
Off-by-one errors in node counting by not properly checking for null/termination conditions
